items.title: Use heapify, not n × heappush
items.avoid: Pushing items one-by-one to build an initial heap from an existing list.
items.why: O(n) vs O(n log n) — meaningful for large initial collections.

items.title: Size-k heap for top-k
items.avoid: Sorting everything just to slice the first k.
items.prefer: Maintain a heap of size k and use `heapq.heapreplace` to swap when a new item beats the current worst-of-top-k.
items.why: O(n log k) ≪ O(n log n) when k is small.

items.title: Add a tie-breaker to your priority tuples
items.prefer: Insert a monotonic counter between priority and payload.
items.why: Prevents `TypeError` on ties and keeps insertion order stable.
items.example:
```python
import itertools, heapq
seq = itertools.count()
heapq.heappush(pq, (priority, next(seq), payload))
```

items.title: Lazy deletion for arbitrary removes
items.avoid: Calling `pq.remove(x)` followed by `heapify(pq)` in hot paths.
items.prefer: Mark deleted items in a side set and skip them when popped.
items.why: Keeps push/pop O(log n); avoids an O(n) scan + O(n) rebuild on every removal.
